title: Authenticated Crawls, Sessions, Tenant Isolation, and Privacy · Contract Lens: Secret Lifecycle
summary: Credentials are copied into configs, sessions cross origins and users, logout links execute, sensitive pages enter shared caches, and retained reports leak personal data. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler ts secret lifecycle increment in least-authority session and privacy policy. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Acquire secrets through approved channels, avoid source and logs, scope them minimally, rotate exposure, and destroy temporary material.
Require explicit owner and account authorization, purpose, role, environment, page scope, time window, and revocation for authenticated access.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Competency probe: Acquire secrets through approved channels, avoid source and logs, scope them minimally, rotate exposure, and destroy temporary material.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Secret Lifecycle model · Theory Secret Lifecycle, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Environment variables make secrets inherently safe.
